rawInput:
w12d1

push press
- not eccentric
- 40x10 felt good; the should thing still bothering me but lowering the weight feels the arm at 90 degree a line that goes from hand to shoulder - wtf is that? also raising thing at 90 deg is getting hard even light things; squeezing right hand also triggering the weakness and pain?! just got a better mattress but this isn't resolving; shoulders super tight
- ribs down is really hard on second set, starting to flare 40x10
- 40x8 and form went to shit; closed with two more after dropping

how come we don't do shoulder shrugs? they feel pretty good tbh with weights; shoulders feel really tight even with rolling

lat raises
- slow eccentric lat raises super hard!
- 15x20
- 15x20 slowing down and getting tough

chest press machine
- switched to this bc available
- slow eccentrics
- 165x10
- 165x10 way easier when this is third!! still not doing four holding at 3
- 165x10 slow eccentric to 10 still very hard and struggled last three but got full form

incline db press
- warm so going to 45
- slow paced but not 2s eccentric
- 45x10 felt weirdly good no pain
- 45x10
- 45x10
- last set was hard squeezed at bottom too and did slowly, struggled at and but finished

kinesis cable fly
- 8x12 first set really crackly but felt really really good after
- really good second and third sets too felt great - this is stretching and relaxing everything

cable column lat raise
- if i lead with elbow and focus on back, i feel it in the elbow even tho not using it at all?
- left side too easy right side doable but feeling it in the shoulder (im right handed!) third one was probably the best / felt the okayest

incline lat
- cable column
- 50 too easy x15
- 70x15 felt like good working weight not eccentric
notes: **New right-shoulder symptom pattern (push press) — flag for monitoring:** user reports a 'line from hand to shoulder' sensation at the 90° arm position when lowering, raising arm to 90° is getting hard 'even light things,' and **squeezing the right hand triggers weakness + pain in the shoulder.** New mattress isn't resolving. Shoulders 'super tight' even with rolling. Push press still hit 40x10/10/8 (rib flare set 2, form broke set 3 — dropped, added 2 more). **User question (worth a discussion note):** 'how come we don't do shoulder shrugs? they feel pretty good tbh with weights' — currently no shrugs in the program. **W12 chest-press fatigue test PASSED:** 165 x 10 x 3 clean at the new 3rd-station placement. User direct quote: 'way easier when this is third!! still not doing four holding at 3.' The fatigue-ordering hypothesis from W11D1 holds — the order tradeoff was the issue, not the load. Set 3 'struggled last three but got full form.' No drop to 150 needed. **Incline DB up to 45** (warm right shoulder, prescription allowed): 45x10 x 3, 'felt weirdly good no pain,' last set hard but completed slow. **Kinesis cable fly Kinesis 8** — first set 'really crackly' then 'felt great, stretching and relaxing everything' for sets 2-3. The spotlight cue (stretch is the stimulus) confirmed. **Cable lat raise reflection:** user noticed 'if i lead with elbow and focus on back, i feel it in the elbow even tho not using it at all' — interesting cue interaction; left too easy, right doable but feeling it in the shoulder, third set was best. **Incline overhead tri ext on cable column at 70** (user correction: 'incline lat' was actually 'incline overhead tri'). 50 too easy → 70 working weight, not eccentric. First cable-column working number for this lift (W10/W11 were Kinesis 4-5). Straight-arm pulldown done as prescribed ('fine,' no specifics).
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

sets.notes: Not eccentric. Felt good. Shoulder thing still bothering — 'line from hand to shoulder' at 90° when lowering. Raising to 90° hard 'even light things.' **Squeezing right hand triggers weakness + pain.** New mattress isn't resolving. Shoulders super tight.
Ribs down really hard on second set — starting to flare.
Form went to shit; closed with two more after dropping (dropped weight or stopped+restarted unclear).

exercises.notes: **New shoulder symptoms — see top-level notes.** Push press load held at 40; rep target hit 10/10/8 (W11 = 10/10/8 identical). User asks: 'how come we don't do shoulder shrugs? they feel pretty good with weights.' Worth a discussion note in NOTES.md.

sets.notes: Slow eccentric — super hard.
Slowing down and getting tough.

exercises.notes: **Only 2 sets logged** (vs prescribed 3). Slow eccentric the focus — that's the W12 spotlight cue. User confirmed the slow tempo is the right stimulus ('super hard').

sets.notes: Slow eccentrics.
**Way easier when this is third!!** Still 3 sets, not 4.
Slow eccentric to 10 still very hard, struggled last 3 but got full form.

exercises.notes: **W12 fatigue-test PASSED.** Chest press 165 x 10 x 3 all clean at the 3rd-station placement (switched in because the machine was available — happened to land in roughly the prescribed slot). User direct quote: 'way easier when this is third!! still not doing four holding at 3.' The W11D1 fatigue hypothesis (it was the order, not the load) is confirmed. No drop to 150 needed.

sets.notes: Warm so going to 45 (up from W11's 40). Slow paced but not 2s eccentric. **Felt weirdly good no pain.**
Last set hard — squeezed at bottom too, did slowly, struggled at end but finished.

exercises.notes: **Up to 45 (from W11's 40) — prescribed 'try 45 only if right shoulder warm + quiet on set 1' was a green light.** All 3 sets clean. 'Weirdly good no pain' is a notable shift.

sets.notes: First set really crackly but felt really really good after.
Felt great — stretching and relaxing everything.
Felt great.

exercises.notes: **Spotlight confirmed.** First-set crackle then progressive opening across sets 2-3 — the 'stretch is the stimulus' framing held up. User: 'this is stretching and relaxing everything.' Protect this lift's placement.

sets.notes: Left side too easy.
Right side doable but feeling it in the shoulder (user is right-handed).
**Third one was probably the best / felt the okayest.**

exercises.notes: **Cue observation:** user noticed 'if i lead with elbow and focus on back, i feel it in the elbow even tho not using it at all.' Worth a form-cue note. Left side too easy, right side feeling shoulder (right-handed) — same asymmetry pattern as W11D1. Weights not specified in raw input.

sets.notes: Too easy.
**Felt like good working weight, not eccentric.**

exercises.notes: **User correction:** raw input said 'incline lat' — actually was incline overhead tri. Cable column 50→70 progression. **First cable-column working number for this lift** (W11D1 was Kinesis 5, W10D1 was Kinesis 4). Going forward, cable column 70 = working weight when the column is available.

sets.notes: Done as prescribed — user: 'straight pulldown fine,' no specifics needed.

rawInput:
w12d3

pull ups
- 8 super slow good ones felt great for first time in a long time 
- 6 slow eccentrics and stopped out
- 6 slow eccentrics and stopped out again
- still feel strength is catching back up or at least stamina
- another 5 slow ones and done
- all on a straight bar btw wanted to get basics working

chest bd row
35x10 slow feel good
did three sets felt good and easy could go more num or heavier


rows
60/70 were easy
80 were hard on eccentric but did the entire set! felt super hard tho but didn't break

incline curl
- lmao left side struggles to hit 10 on first set : slow eccentrics tho
- second set right side also struggles - definitely weak on this front 
- third set 8 good ones and two supported for both sides but didn't check notes lol oops


reverse fly
on 20 - 22.5 don't exist at this gym 
- tried fully face down on bench- super hard 12
- 30deg whoa hard but this is way better than hunched over version 12 
- last set fully complete but these feel good / engage the right things


lat pull down
140x12
140x12 somehow really strong on these; slow eccentric, realized did too many
140x10 two down two up slow felt great but stopped out at 10 will prob be painful tomorrow?? hope not

face pulls
- following instructions carefully holy shit like another exercise
- doing them slowly with a hold
- 20x42.5
- 20x42.5 completely different parts burning 
- 20x42.5 did all of them but need to continue repping these this way this is great, neck feels less engaged 

one arm curl
- 160x12 felt easy so went 170- these are harder but definitely feeling the right pec / chest 
- but 170 felt fine for both, approaching working weight; 180 would def be hard but doable
notes: Pull day went well. Pull-ups all on **straight bar** by choice ('wanted to get basics working') — 8 super slow + 6 + 6 + 5 = 25 total, all slow eccentrics. User: 'felt great for first time in a long time' on the first set, and 'still feel strength is catching back up or at least stamina.' Different rep distribution than W11D3's mixed-grip 7/7/7/6 — fewer reps on the heavier sets, but cleaner tempo. **Chest-supported row: 35 was available this week** (W11 forced to 30) — 35x10x3 'felt good and easy, could go more num or heavier.' Real working weight 35 is now confirmed clean — open to next push. **DB row pyramid 60/70/80 held form at 80** — W11 broke form on 80 with momentum, W12 'felt super hard tho but didn't break.' Form-first cue won at 80. **Incline curl: both sides weak today, not just left.** Quote: 'left side struggles to hit 10 on first set... second set right side also struggles - definitely weak on this front... third set 8 good ones and two supported for both sides but didn't check notes lol oops.' User flagged self that they didn't follow the 'left-first' protocol from the workout. Possible fatigue: pull-ups all-slow + chest-supported row + DB row pyramid before this. **Reverse fly spotlight working — reverse-incline variant clicked.** User tried 'fully face down on bench' (super hard, 12 reps) then settled at 30° incline: 'whoa hard but this is way better than hunched over version, 12.' Last set complete, 'feels good / engage the right things.' 22.5 still doesn't exist at this gym — 20 with reverse-incline form is the working setup. **Lat pulldown back to regular machine at 140** (no forced sub this week). 140x12, 140x12 ('somehow really strong, slow eccentric, realized did too many'), 140x10 'two down two up slow' — user said 'felt great but stopped out at 10, will prob be painful tomorrow?? hope not.' **Face pull spotlight: cue change was transformative.** User: 'following instructions carefully holy shit like another exercise... doing them slowly with a hold... 20x42.5 x3 sets... completely different parts burning... need to continue repping these this way this is great, **neck feels less engaged**.' The neck-disengagement is exactly what the spotlight was trying to drill. Hold the 42.5 + cue work going forward. **High cable curl: 170 working weight, headed to 180.** 160x12 'felt easy so went 170 - these are harder but definitely feeling the right pec/chest, but 170 felt fine for both, approaching working weight; 180 would def be hard but doable.' User note about 'feeling the right pec/chest' is interesting — at high-cable-curl setup (upper arm parallel to floor at shoulder level), the pec stabilizes the arm position; not a pure bicep load. **🔆 SHRUG HYPOTHESIS GOT A STRONG FIRST SIGNAL** (35lb behind-back DB shrugs, W12D1 prescription introduction): user direct quote — 'felt some kind of crackly pop and then it's like everything aligned, in the middle of the first set; these felt super aligning and I think we should do these way more / and/or add more of this kind / whatever this affects, do more of this.' Exactly the pattern the discussion-doc hypothesis predicted (upper-trap-dominant pattern releases when lower/mid trap loads). Crackly pop is consistent with fascial/scapular unsticking — same family as the foam roller release. **One data point but strong** — continue shrugs into W13, accelerate the rotation. Cable crunch + cool-down both completed without specifics — user followup: 'all the other stuff not mentioned went great btw / no notes.'
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

sets.notes: Super slow good ones — 'felt great for first time in a long time'.
Slow eccentrics, stopped out.
Slow eccentrics, stopped out again.
Another 5 slow ones and done.

exercises.notes: All on **straight bar** by choice — user: 'wanted to get basics working.' Different from W11D3's mixed-grip 7/7/7/6. Total reps 25 (vs W11D3's 27) but all-slow-eccentric throughout, not the W11 mix. User: 'still feel strength is catching back up or at least stamina.'

sets.notes: Slow, felt good.
All 3 sets — 'felt good and easy, could go more num or heavier'.

exercises.notes: 35lb available this week (W11 forced to 30 because 35s weren't around). 35 clean and easy — push number or reps next week is open.

sets.notes: Easy.
Easy.
Hard on eccentric but did the entire set — 'felt super hard tho but didn't break'.

exercises.notes: Pyramid 60/70/80 held. W11D3 broke form at 80 with momentum — W12 didn't break. The form-first cue stuck at 80.

sets.notes: Left side struggles to hit 10 on first set; slow eccentrics though.
Second set: right side also struggles — 'definitely weak on this front.'
Third set: 8 good ones and two supported for both sides. User: 'didn't check notes lol oops' — didn't follow the prescribed left-first protocol.

exercises.notes: **Different pattern from W11D3** — W11 had left weak, right strict at 10. W12 both sides weak today. Possible cumulative fatigue from all-slow pull-ups + chest-supported row 35 + DB row pyramid all before incline curl. User self-flagged they skipped the left-first protocol.

sets.weight: 20lb (22.5 'don't exist at this gym' — confirmed equipment limit)
sets.notes: **Tried fully face-down on bench** — super hard.
**Settled at 30° incline (chest-down on incline bench)** — 'whoa hard but this is WAY BETTER than hunched over version'.
Last set fully complete — 'these feel good / engage the right things'.

exercises.notes: **Reverse-incline variant working as the W7+ note predicted.** User experimented with two angles this session: fully face-down (super hard, 12) vs. 30° chest-down on incline bench (the win — 'way better than hunched over version'). 30° is the working setup. 22.5 doesn't exist at this gym — 20 is the permanent working weight here.

exercises.equipment: regular lat pulldown (available this week — not forced to dual pulley)

sets.notes: Slow eccentric — 'somehow really strong on these, realized did too many' (intended fewer reps).
**'Two down two up slow' tempo** — 2s descent + 2s pull. 'Felt great but stopped out at 10, will prob be painful tomorrow?? hope not.'

exercises.notes: Regular lat pulldown available this week (W11D3 was forced to dual pulley at 70/side). 140 confirmed working at the regular machine. Tempo experiment 'two down two up slow' on set 3 — user liked it but worried about next-day soreness.

sets.notes: 'Completely different parts burning' — different muscle activation pattern vs prior weeks.
'Did all of them but need to continue repping these this way this is great, **neck feels less engaged**'.

exercises.notes: **Spotlight cue change was transformative.** User: 'following instructions carefully holy shit like another exercise... doing them slowly with a hold.' All 3 sets clean at 42.5. The **neck-disengagement** observation is exactly what the elbows-high + external-rotation spotlight was trying to drill. Hold 42.5 + the cue protocol going forward. This is the form-first thesis in action — same load, different lift entirely.

exercises.equipment: cable column (one arm at a time)

sets.notes: Felt easy → ramped up.

**Harder but felt the right pec / chest** (user note — at high-cable setup with arm parallel to floor at shoulder level, pec stabilizes). 170 felt fine for both arms — 'approaching working weight; 180 would def be hard but doable.'

exercises.notes: **Working weight ramps: W11 150 → W12 prescribed 160 → W12 actual 170.** User projects 180 next as the working stretch. Pec/chest sensation at the high-cable setup is positional (upper arm parallel at shoulder level stabilizes via pec), not a sign the exercise is wrong.

sets.notes: Done — user didn't flag anything. Followup user clarification: 'all the other stuff not mentioned went great btw / no notes.'

exercises.notes: Completed without specifics — user confirmed 'no notes' on followup. Default to prescribed 130 unless user reports differently.

sets.notes: **Felt GREAT — crackly pop mid-first-set, then everything aligned.** User direct quote: 'felt some kind of crackly pop and then it's like everything aligned, in the middle of the first set; these felt super aligning.'
Continued — 'felt super aligning.'
User: 'I think we should do these way more / and/or add more of this kind / whatever this affects, do more of this.'

exercises.notes: **MAJOR FINDING — W12D1 shrug hypothesis got a strong first signal.** First session of the new behind-back DB shrug protocol. 35lb (mid-range of the prescribed 30-40 start window). Crackly pop mid-set-1 followed by 'everything aligned' — that's the exact pattern the shoulder-shrugs-discussion.md hypothesis predicted (upper-trap-dominant pattern releases when the lower/mid trap loads up). User explicitly wants MORE of this kind of work. **One data point — strong but needs 2-3 sessions to confirm durability vs first-session novelty.** Direction confirmed: continue shrugs in W13 pull (even though deload), add trap-3 raise rotation as planned, accelerate farmer carry intro to W13D1 push (already in plan). If the grip → right-shoulder coupling reduces over the next 2 weeks, hypothesis is confirmed functional (not structural). The crackly pop specifically is consistent with fascial/scapular tissue unsticking — same family as the foam roller and feet-on-roller releases.

sets.weight: (prescribed 5-min sequence: doorframe pec + dead hang + supine twist + 90/90 + child's pose)
sets.notes: Done — user: 'all the other stuff not mentioned went great btw / no notes.'

exercises.notes: **First W12+ pull-day cool-down completed.** No specific feedback — interpret as the block landed without friction (would have surfaced complaints otherwise). The 5-min block stays as the permanent closing pattern.

rawInput:
w12d4

why is the tgu talking about racking?? isn't it straight arm? wtf?
otherwise tgu on both sides feel fine now!

all the flows are marked weird it's a flow not reps - please correct it to be a flow instead of each part separate

Otherwise did all the d4 exercises and it was fine; like the more skills stuff, love the figure 8 flows, it's like a bit of strength + cardio + very entertaining, definitely did it more than written (did it to music)
notes: Light Thursday KB day — went smoothly. **TGU both sides feel fine now** — the W12 left-side spotlight goal is met: the left is no longer the weak link, no asymmetry the user flagged. **Two workout-content corrections the user surfaced (both fixed):** (1) the TGU spotlight cue said 'clean the bell to a stable rack before the get-up' — user correctly flagged the TGU is a straight-arm overhead lift, the arm stays locked vertical the whole way with no rack hold during the get-up. The clean-to-rack is only a brief setup entry before pressing overhead. Fixed in fb-w12-kb-cond.json (3 spots) + form-cues-2026.md. (2) The OTD flow-block had each move marked with per-exercise reps ('2 each side'), reading as 5 separate stations — user wanted it framed as one continuous flow. Restructured: reps removed, set note now describes the flowing sequence. **Figure-8 → tactical clean flow was a big hit** — user: 'bit of strength + cardio + very entertaining,' did MORE rounds than the 2 prescribed, did it to music. The rotationPlan condition ('figure-8 flow stays in rotation if W12D4 lands well') is met — keep it in. User wants more skill/flow-style work generally. Everything else (banded prep, OTD flow, windmill, cooldown) done, no issues.

sets.notes: **Both sides feel fine now** — left no longer the weak link. W12 left-side spotlight goal met.

exercises.notes: Left-side checkpoint spotlight landed — user reports no asymmetry, 'both sides feel fine.' Separately flagged the workout's 'clean to a stable rack' cue as wrong for a straight-arm lift (corrected).

sets.notes: Did more rounds than the 2 prescribed — 'definitely did it more than written (did it to music).'

exercises.notes: **Big hit.** User: 'love the figure 8 flows, it's like a bit of strength + cardio + very entertaining.' First run of the new @kettleclubfam complex (light version) — lands well, stays in rotation. User wants more skill/flow work like this.

rawInput:
w12d5

ql and psoas extremely tight from yesterday oops

crossing legs and leaning one way then the other is really helpful in loosening up - should add to notes officially

warm up fine

75x10 kind of heavy didn't get full range
75x6 realized did too many last time; did fewer but lower, felt really good actually
75x6 also felt pretty good, doing fewer lets me do it way better and not fear the reps being too many
75x8 pyramiding up felt good actually did 8 and felt strong still; could do more pyramid next time to build up courage

incline rdl bc equipment busy
- 25lb x3
- last set super hard but did all sets and reps, slow eccentrics; walking is hard at the end!
- feels great no probs

leg press
- do feel like my ql muscles will give in at some point today lol good to have three plates on leg press
- full rom on these goes literally knee to chest like frogger; pushing to prevent lower back from lifting
- it's like a ql massage…. not hard at all but feel the intersection of super weak muscles plus leg muscles which apparent don't seem to care about this weight. weird. full rom at lighter is the way to go, for a while until the core muscles get untucked
- did all sets fine and full rom, will prob feel this as a deep stretch not a workout later

calf raise is on the incline leg press machine your instructions don't make sense; purely bottom half so ball is on the platform
- slow count is incredibly hard lol
- 3plates x15
-

[followup] oh anything not written was done btw
notes: Legs day, solid. **QL + psoas extremely tight coming in** — from W12D4 (KB day): the figure-8 flow done 'more than written' plus TGU hip-bridge / wide-stance work. User 'oops' — acknowledged overdoing the figure-8 volume. **User mobility discovery — wants it on the record:** crossing the legs and leaning side to side loosens the QL/psoas well. Added to NOTES.md; good candidate to rotate into legs-day warmup/cooldown. **Squat: prescribed 75x8x4, user self-pyramided to 75 x 10/6/6/8.** Real discovery — fewer reps per set let the depth and form improve ('did fewer but lower, felt really good') and removed the fear of overshooting the count. Set 1 at 10 was 'kind of heavy, didn't get full range'; the 6-rep sets were cleaner and deeper. User wants to pyramid more next week to 'build up courage.' **Incline RDL — order shuffled** (done before leg press because equipment was busy; not a swap, incline RDL is the prescription). 25lb x15 x3, slow eccentrics, all sets + reps; last set super hard, 'walking is hard at the end' but 'feels great no probs.' **Leg press: 3 plates, full ROM** — 'literally knee to chest like frogger,' actively pushing to keep the lower back from lifting off the pad. User framed it as 'a QL massage… not hard at all' on the leg muscles, but the deep ROM works the weak core/QL. User's own takeaway: full ROM at lighter load is the way to go 'until the core muscles get untucked.' **Calf raise: user flagged the workout instructions as wrong** — the plate-under-foot / toes-hanging-off cue doesn't match the incline leg press machine; correct setup is balls of the feet on the bottom platform edge, bottom-half ROM. Instructions corrected in fb-w12-legs.json + form-cues doc. Slow count 'incredibly hard' at 3 plates. **Seated leg curl (the other W12 spotlight) was not detailed in the input; user followup confirmed 'anything not written was done' — logged at the prescribed 90, clean.**
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

sets.notes: Kind of heavy — didn't get full range of motion.
Realized did too many on set 1; did fewer reps but went lower — 'felt really good actually.'
Also felt good — fewer reps lets the form be way better, no fear of the rep count running too long.
Pyramided back up — felt good, did 8 and 'felt strong still.'

exercises.notes: Prescribed 75x8x4 — user self-pyramided to 10/6/6/8. **Discovery: dropping reps per set improved depth + form** ('did fewer but lower, felt really good') and removed the fear of overshooting. User wants to pyramid more next week to 'build up courage.' 75/side held cleanly — squat is solid at this load.

sets.notes: Slow eccentrics.
Slow eccentrics.
Last set super hard but completed all reps. 'Walking is hard at the end' — 'feels great no probs.'

exercises.notes: Order shuffled — done before leg press because equipment was busy (not a swap; incline RDL is the prescription). 25lb held, dead-slow, all 3 sets + 15 reps completed.

sets.notes: Full ROM — 'knee to chest like frogger.'
All sets full ROM. User: 'will prob feel this as a deep stretch not a workout later.'

exercises.notes: 3 plates, full ROM — 'literally knee to chest like frogger,' pushing to keep the lower back from lifting off the pad. User: 'it's like a QL massage… not hard at all but feel the intersection of super weak [core/QL] muscles plus leg muscles which don't care about this weight.' User's framing: full ROM at lighter load is the way to go until the core muscles 'get untucked.' Depth push (the W12 prescription) landed — all sets full ROM.

sets.notes: Slow count 'incredibly hard.' Balls of feet on the bottom platform edge, bottom-half ROM (user's correction to the workout's instructions).

exercises.notes: **User flagged the workout instructions as wrong** — the plate-under-foot / toes-hanging-off cue doesn't match the incline leg press machine. Correct setup: balls of the feet on the bottom platform edge, heels off, bottom-half ROM. Fixed in fb-w12-legs.json + form-cues doc. Slow eccentric 'incredibly hard' at 3 plates. Logged 3 plates x15; user followup: 'anything not written was done' — all 3 prescribed sets completed.

sets.notes: Done as prescribed — user followup: 'anything not written was done.'

exercises.notes: Not detailed in the raw input; user confirmed on followup that 'anything not written was done.' Done at the prescribed 90 (corrected ankle placement, 5/m/0/1). No issues flagged — interpret as the W11D5 → W12 baseline held clean.
